name: Multi-API Data Aggregator — REST Client
scenario: Build a data aggregator that calls multiple simulated REST APIs: a product catalog API, a pricing API, and an inventory API. Aggregates all three responses into a unified product record. Demonstrates GET, POST, header auth, JSON parsing, and error handling — the complete HttpClient skill set. Since real external APIs require live network access unavailable in the exercise environment, all HTTP calls use convincing simulations that demonstrate the identical patterns.
description: A REST API client that aggregates data from three simulated endpoints, handles auth, parses JSON responses with Jackson, gracefully degrades on 404, retries on 429, and writes the aggregated results to a JSON report file.

core.description: `ApiClient` class with: static final HttpClient singleton (configured with timeout), static final ObjectMapper singleton. Helper method `buildGetRequest(String url, String token)` that sets Authorization Bearer header and Accept: application/json.

Three simulated API responses (mock the body strings as would be returned by real APIs):
- GET /products/{id} → product name, category, description
- GET /pricing/{id} → current price, originalPrice, discount%
- GET /inventory/{id} → quantityAvailable, warehouseLocation, inStock
`fetchProduct(String productId)` — calls all three simulated endpoints, parses JSON with Jackson, builds a unified `ProductDetail` object or ObjectNode combining all three responses.
Handle three status scenarios: 200 (parse), 404 (return partial with defaults), 429 (retry once after 200ms delay, then give up with logged warning).
Fetch 5 products (P001-P005). P003 simulates a 404 on inventory. Aggregate results into a List and print a formatted summary table.
Write all results to `product_report.json` using Jackson pretty printer. File includes timestamp, total count, available count, and array of product details.
POST a search request to `/search` with a JSON body `{"category":"Electronics","maxPrice":5000}`. Parse and print the simulated result count.

bonus.description: Add a `RateLimiter` helper: tracks request count per second (AtomicInteger + scheduled reset). If > 10 requests/sec, sleep 100ms before sending. Demonstrates combining concurrency (Topic 10.4) with HTTP.

Add response caching: ConcurrentHashMap<String, CachedResponse> where CachedResponse holds body + timestamp. If cached and < 60 seconds old, return cached body instead of calling the API. Print 'CACHE HIT' or 'CACHE MISS' for each call.

Add a mock authentication flow: POST to `/auth/token` with `{"username":"demo","password":"demo123"}`, receive a Bearer token, then use that token for all subsequent requests. Simulate token expiry after 3 calls and re-authenticate.

data_to_use:
Products: P001(Mouse), P002(Keyboard), P003(Monitor—404 on inventory), P004(USB Hub), P005(Headphones)
API token: 'test-bearer-token-xyz'

Simulated responses:
P001: name=Wireless Mouse, price=799, qty=50
P002: name=Keyboard, price=1999, qty=12
P003: name=Monitor (inventory 404 → inStock=false, qty=0)
P004: name=USB Hub, price=499, qty=60
P005: name=Headphones, price=2999, qty=25
example_output:
=== Product Data Aggregator ===
Fetching 5 products...

P001: Wireless Mouse    ₹799    50 in stock  ✓
P002: Keyboard         ₹1999    12 in stock  ✓
P003: Monitor         ₹18999    [inventory unavailable] ✗
P004: USB Hub           ₹499    60 in stock  ✓
P005: Headphones       ₹2999    25 in stock  ✓

Search POST → found 3 products under ₹5000

=== Summary ===
Total: 5  |  In stock: 4  |  Unavailable: 1
Report written: product_report.json

criteria: Correct field extraction with Jackson (5 pts)
POST with JSON body + parse response (10 pts)
Valid pretty-printed JSON output file (10 pts)

ai_policy_explanation: Limited to 5 hints. Key patterns: simulate response by returning a JSON string directly (same parsing logic works against real HTTP). For 404 simulation: return null from simulate method, check null in fetchProduct. For POST: BodyPublishers.ofString(mapper.writeValueAsString(requestNode)). Report file: Files.writeString(Path.of('product_report.json'), mapper.writerWithDefaultPrettyPrinter().writeValueAsString(reportNode)).
submission_instructions: Save as ApiAggregator.java. Push to GitHub. Comment: 'HttpClient REST API aggregation with error handling'. Note: exercises use simulated responses — real HTTP calls work identically.
tips_for_success: Simulate API: method returns String (mock body) or null (mock 404)
HttpClient singleton: `private static final HttpClient HTTP = HttpClient.newBuilder().connectTimeout(Duration.ofSeconds(10)).build()`
For real calls: `String body = HTTP.send(req, BodyHandlers.ofString()).body()`
Report: ObjectNode with timestamp, count, ArrayNode of product ObjectNodes
